key_points: An equation is a mathematical statement showing that two expressions are equal
A variable is an unknown value represented by a letter (e.g., $x, y, z$)
The Left Hand Side (LHS) must always equal the Right Hand Side (RHS)
An equation remains the same if the LHS and RHS are interchanged
examples: Expression: $2x + 3$ (Note: This is NOT an equation as it lacks an '=' sign)
formulas: Solution $ightarrow$ The value of the variable that satisfies the equation
exam_tips: Identify the variable first before attempting to solve
Remember that 'is' or 'gives' in a word problem usually means '='
Don't confuse an algebraic expression with an equation
Start with simple one-step equations to build your confidence
